hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.ar:
2364  -  وَّ رَّوَّى صَّق ْوَّإن ُّ عَّن ْ مَّن ْصُّورِ ن ْن ِ جَّار ِمٍ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ُّهْذِى َّ لَّن َّا طَّي ْ ز  مَّذ ْن ُّوح  ن ِمَّك َّه َّ  
ق َّا َّكَّلَّهُّ إ َّهْلُّن َّا ق َّق َّال  ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Ibn Muskan narrated from Ibrahim ibn Maymun:   
I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}, "A man plucked the feathers of a pigeon from the pigeons of 
the Haram."  
Imam {a.s} replied: "He should give charity to a needy person and with the hand that 
plucked the feathers, for he has caused it pain."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 235 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 453 • Al-
Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 113 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 153 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2363

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.ar:
2365  -  وَّ رَّوَّى صَّق ْوَّإن ُّ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ سِن َّان ٍ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم لص َّي ْذُّ ق ِى  إَّلْحَّزَّمِ وَّ 
إ ِن ْ صِي ذَّ ق ِى  إَّلْحِل
hadiths.en:
Safwan narrated from Mansur ibn Hazim:   
I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}, "A slaughtered bird was gifted to us in Mecca, and our family 
ate it."  
Imam {a.s} replied: "The people of Mecca see no harm in it."  
I asked: "What do you say about it?"   
Imam {a.s} said: "They are liable for its price."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 236 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 376 
• Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 213 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 113 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 424 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, 
Vol. 13, p. 25 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 39 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2364

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.ar:
2366  - وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلن  َّص ْزُّ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ سِن َّان ٍ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول ى  جَّمَّامِ مَّك َّه َّ إَّلط َّي ْزُّ 
إَّلْأ َّهْلِى  ُّ مِن ْ جَّمَّامِ إَّلْحَّزَّمِ مَّن ْ ذ َّن َّحَّ مِن ْهُّ طَّي ْزإً ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ إ َّن ْ ي َّي َّصَّذ َّق َّ ن ِصَّذَّ ق َّه ٍ إ َّق ْص َّلَّ مِن ْ ي َّمَّن ِهِ ق َّا ِن ْ كَّان َّ مُّحْزِماً ق َّس َّاة   عَّن ْ 
كُّلِّ طَّي ْزٍ.
hadiths.en:
Safwan narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan:   
Abu Abdullah {a.s} said: "Hunting is not to be slaughtered in the Sacred Sanctuary 
(Haram), even if it was hunted in the permissible (Hil)."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 118 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 
425 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 431 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2365

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.en:
Al-Nadr narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan:   
I heard Abu Abdullah {a.s} say: "For the pigeons of Mecca and the domesticated birds 
from the pigeons of the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram), whoever slaughters a bird from 
them must give charity greater than its value. If the person was in the state of Ihram, 
then a sheep is required for each bird."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 235 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 111 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 23 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2366
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.en:
Muawiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a domesticated bird that 
entered the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram).  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It should not be taken or touched because Allah {SWT}, the Almighty, 
says: 'And whoever enters it shall be safe.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2367

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.ar:
2369  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ مُّسْكَّان َّ عَّن ْ ي َّز ِي ذَّ ن ْن ِ ج َّلِي ق َّه َّ ق َّالَّ : كَّان َّ ق ِى  ج َّان ِث ِ ن َّي ْي ِى  مِكْي َّل  كَّان َّ ق ِي هِ ن َّي ْص َّي َّان ِ مِن ْ   جَّمَّامِ 
إَّلْحَّزَّمِ ق َّذ َّهَّث َّ ع ُّلاَّمِى  ق َّكَّث  َّ إَّلْمِكْي َّلَّ وَّ هُّوَّ لأَّ ي َّعْلَّمُّ إ َّن  َّ ق ِي هِ ن َّي ْص َّي َّي ْن ِ ق َّكَّسَّزَّهُّمَّا ق َّح َّزَّج ْث ُّ  ق َّلَّق ِي ث ُّ عَّن ْذَّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن َّ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ 
ق َّذ َّكَّزْب ُّ ذ َّلِك َّ لَّهُّ ق َّق َّالَّ ي َّصَّذ َّق ْ ن ِكَّق  َّي ْن ِ مِن ْ ذَّق ِي ق ٍ ق َّالَّ ق َّلَّق ِي ث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ  ن َّعْذُّ ق َّا َّج ْن َّزْي ُّهُّ ق َّق َّالَّ لِى  عَّلَّي ْهِ 
إَّلس َّلاَّم عِمُّ ن ِهِ جَّمَّامَّ إَّلْحَّزَّمِ 
إ َّج َّذ َّ عَّن ْ إ ن َّان ِهِ عَّلَّي ْهِمُّ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ.
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim asked one of the Imams {a.s}  about a gazelle entering 
the Sacred Sanctuary (Haram).  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It should not be taken or touched because Allah {SWT}, the Almighty, 
says: 'And whoever enters it shall be safe.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 362 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 
104 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 557 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 75 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2368

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.ar:
2370 - وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ س ِهَّاب ِ ن ْن ِ عَّن ْذِ رَّن ِّهِ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِن ِّى  إ َّي َّسَّح َّزُّ ن ِق ِ زَّإح ٍ إ ُّي ِى َّ ن ِهَّا مِن ْ ع َّي ْزِ 
مَّك َّه َّ ق َّي ُّذ ْن َّحُّ ق ِى  إَّلْحَّزَّمِ ق َّا َّي َّسَّح َّزُّ ن ِهَّا ق َّق َّال ا عَّلِمْث َّ إ َّن  َّ مَّا إ َّذْج َّلْث َّ ن ِهِ إَّلْحَّزَّمَّ جَّي  اً ق َّق َّذْ  
جَّزُّمَّ عَّلَّي ْك َّ ذ َّن ْحُّهُّ وَّ إ ِمْسَّاكُّه
hadiths.en:
Ibn Maskan narrated from Yazid ibn Khalifa, who said:   
"There was a basket near my house containing two eggs of the Sacred Sanctuary’s 
pigeons. My servant, unaware of the eggs, overturned the basket, breaking them. I 
went out and met Abdullah ibn al-Hasan, and I mentioned this to him.  
He said: 'Give in charity two handfuls of flour.'  
Later, I met Abu Abdullah {a.s} and informed him.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'He must pay the price of two birds, feeding the pigeons of the Sacred 
Sanctuary with it.'  
I then met Abdullah ibn al-Hasan and informed him of this.  
He said: 'He {a.s} has spoken the truth; follow it, for He {a.s} has taken this from his 
forefathers {a.s}.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 236 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 115 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2369

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.ar:
2371  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ جُّمْزَّإن َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  ن ْن ِ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ 
عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ن ِالْحَّزَّمِ ق َّزَّإ ن ِى  إ ُّوذ ِى  إَّلْح َّطَّاطِي ف َّ ق َّق َّال  لأَّ ي ُّو ْذ ِهِن  َّ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّن  َّ لأَّ ي ُّو ْذ ِي ن َّ س َّي ْن اً
hadiths.en:
Shihab ibn Abd Rabbihi narrated:   
"I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}: 'I partake in Suhoor with chicks brought from outside 
Mecca, which are slaughtered in the Sacred Sanctuary, and I partake in Suhoor with 
them.'  
Imam {a.s} replied: 'What an evil Suhoor is your Suhoor! Do you not know that whatever 
you bring into the Sacred Sanctuary alive becomes forbidden for you to slaughter and 
to retain?'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 119 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 31 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2370

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.ar:
2372   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ِ إَّلْحَّح  َّاح ِ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ ق َّزْج َّي ْ ن ِ مُّسَّزْوَّلَّي ْن ِ ذ َّن َّحْي ُّهُّمَّا 
وَّ إ َّن َّا ن ِمَّك َّه َّ ق َّق َّالَّ لِى إ َّهْلِ مَّك َّه َّ ق َّسَّا َّلَّي ْن ِى  إ َّن ْ إ َّذ ْن َّحَّهُّمَّا ق َّط َّن َّن ْث ُّ إ َّن ِّى   
ن ِالْكُّوق َّه ِ وَّ لَّمْ إ َّذ ْكُّزِ إَّلْحَّزَّمَّ ق َّال ا
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Humran narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s} from his father {a.s}, 
who said:  "I was with Ali ibn al -Husayn {a.s} in the Sacred Sanctuary, and he saw me 
troubling the swallows.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'O my son, do not kill them and do not harm them, for they do not harm 
anything.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 120 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 35 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2371

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.en:
Abdul Rahman ibn al -Hajjaj narrated:  "I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about two 
young birds dressed in feathers that I slaughtered while I was in Mecca.  
Imam {a.s} said to me: 'Why did you slaughter them?'  
I replied: 'A girl from the people of Mecca brought them to me and asked me to 
slaughter them, and I thought I was in Kufa and did not recall the sanctity of the 
Haram.'  
Imam {a.s} said: 'Give charity equivalent to their value.'  
I asked: 'How much?'  
Imam {a.s} said: 'A dirham, which is better than them.'"   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 237 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 117 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2372

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.ar:
2374  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْمُّي َّن  َّى عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّكَّمِ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِع ُّلاَّمٍ لَّن َّا هَّي ِّى ْ لَّن َّا ع َّذَّإءَّن َّا ق َّا َّج َّذ َّ ل ن َّا مِن ْ إ َّطْي َّارِ مَّك َّه َّ 
ق َّذ َّن َّحَّهَّا وَّ طَّن َّح َّهَّا ق َّذَّج َّلْث ُّ عَّلَّى إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّال ن ْهُّن  َّ وَّ إِق ْذِ عَّن ْ كُّلِّ طَّي ْزٍ مِن ْهُّن
hadiths.en:
Zurara narrated:  
He asked (Imam {a.s}) about a man who took a bird from Mecca to Kufa.  
The Imam {a.s} said: "He must return it to Mecca."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2373

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.ar:
2375  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  جَّمْز َّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ ق َّي َّلَّ ط ي ْزإً مِن ْ طُّي ُّورِ 
إَّلْحَّزَّمِ وَّ هُّوَّ مُّحْزِم  ق ِى  إَّلْحَّزَّمِ ق َّق َّال ن ِهِ جَّمَّامَّ إَّلْحَّزَّمِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ ق َّزْج اً ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ 
جَّمَّل  وَّ ق ِي مَّه ُّ إَّلْق َّزْح ِ ن ِصْف ُّ ذِرْهَّمٍ ي َّعْلِف ُّ ن ِهِ جَّمَّامَّ إَّلْحَّزَّمِ
hadiths.en:
Al-Muthanna narrated from Muhammad ibn Abi al-Hakam:  
He said: I told a servant of ours, "Prepare our meal." So he took birds from Mecca, 
slaughtered them, and cooked them.  
I went to Abu Abdullah {a.s}, and Imam {a.s}  said: "Bury them and offer compensation 
(fidya) for each bird among them."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 103 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2374

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.ar:
2376  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال وجاً ق َّذْ ذ ُّن ِحَّ ق ِى  إَّلْحِلِّ  
ي ُّم َّ ج ِى ءَّ ن ِهِ إ ِلَّى إَّلْحَّزَّمِ مَّذ ْن ُّوجاً ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ ن ِهِ لِلْحَّلاَّلِ
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abi Basir, from Abi Abdullah {a.s}:  
Regarding a man who killed a bird of the sanctuary while he was in a state of ihram 
within the Haram,  
Imam {a.s} said: "He must offer a sheep as compensation, and the value of the bird is 
one dirham, with which he should feed the pigeons of the Haram. If it was a chick, then 
he must offer a lamb, and the value of the chick is half a dirham, with which he should 
also feed the pigeons of the Haram."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 770 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 30 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2375

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi narrated from Abi Abdullah {a.s}:  
"Do not purchase [meat] in the Haram unless it has been slaughtered in the Hill 
(outside the Haram) and then brought to the Haram after being slaughtered; in such a 
case, there is no issue with it for someone who is not in a state of ihram."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 425 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
«

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.ar:
2378   -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحَّح  َّاح ِ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم امَّه ِ ذِرْهَّم  وَّ ق ِى   
إَّلْق َّزْح ِ ن ِصْف ُّ ذِرْهَّمٍ وَّ ق ِى  إَّلْن َّي ْص َّه ِ رُّن ُّغُّ ذِرْهَّمٍ
hadiths.en:
Sa'id ibn Abdullah Al -A'raj asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about an ostrich egg that 
he ate while in the Haram.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Give in charity the equivalent of its price."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2377

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON THE PROHIBITION OF HUNTING IN THE
hadiths.en:
Abdul Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj narrated that Abu Abdullah {a.s} said:  
"The value of a pigeon is one dirham, the value of a chick is half a dirham, and the value 
of an egg is a quarter of a dirham."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 234 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 107 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 25

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 65, H. 2378

hadiths.chapter: 66. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMITTED TO BE
hadiths.en:
Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"It is not permissible to slaughter in the Haram except for camels, cows, sheep, and 
chickens."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 367 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 
124 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 125 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 548 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 66, H. 2379

hadiths.chapter: 66. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMITTED TO BE
hadiths.ar:
2381 - وَّ ق َّالَّ ج َّمِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ ذَّر َّإح ٍ وَّ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّسْلِمٍ : سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلذ َّج َّاح ِ  إَّلسِّن ْذِى ِّ ي ُّح ْزَّح ُّ ن ِهِ 
مِن َّ إَّلْحَّزَّمِ ق َّق َّال  
وَّ ق ِى  ج َّن َّزٍ إ ج َّز
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiya ibn Ammar asked Imam {a.s} about Ethiopian chickens.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It is not considered part of the game (sayd); game birds are those 
that fly between the sky and the earth and flap their wings."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 66, H. 2380

hadiths.chapter: 66. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMITTED TO BE
hadiths.en:
Jamil ibn Darraj and Muhammad ibn Muslim reported that Abu Abdullah {a.s} 
was asked about Sindhi chickens being taken out of the Haram.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, because they cannot fly independently."   
In another narration: "They only flutter lightly."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 80 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 66, H. 2381
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMITTED TO BE
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMITTED TO BE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٦، ح ٢٣٨١

hadiths.chapter: 66. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMITTED TO BE
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn al -Sayqal asked Imam {a.s} about the chickens and birds of 
Mecca.  
Imam {a.s} said: "What does not glide (in flight), you may eat it, and what glides, let it go."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 66, H. 2382
